task.name: Backend task queue: reconcile, list, consume
task.objective: Make the backend task queue truthful and actionable: (1) RECONCILE — settle every locally-consumed task whose goal finished (goal done → task resolved, goal failed → task failed) via task-update-status; (2) LIST — render all unresolved tasks; (3) CONSUME (opt-in) — claim tasks and convert them into taskvisor goals, recording the task↔goal mapping so a later run can reconcile them. The lifecycle contract: task-claim is ALWAYS called before any work is taken, and task-update-status ALWAYS marks the outcome.

glossary.term: "list" (default) or "consume" — set by a `consume` token in $ARGUMENTS.
Consume count: integer following the `consume` token. Default 1, hard cap 3.

Optional `category=<c>` and `severity=<s>` tokens in $ARGUMENTS. Validate against the closed sets (category: plan, supervisor, validator, execute, general; severity: critical, warning, info) and reject unknown values naming the allowed ones.
.tmux-cli/task-goals.yaml — the local task↔goal ledger: `mappings: [{task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at}]`. Created on first consume; absence means nothing to reconcile.
.tmux-cli/goals.yaml — read-only here (atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe). NEVER written by this command.

lane-gate.name: solo-lane
lane-gate.title: Solo-lane gate (LANE GATE G1–G5)
lane-gate.decision-rule: ALL of G1–G4 must hold AND the demotion veto must not fire → lane=solo; any miss or a fired veto → full. Evaluated UPSTREAM only; executing agents never self-declare.
lane-gate.criterion: Exactly one unit of work (single backend task at consume; exactly 1 pending task at plan step 3d).
Every green-state check is a deterministic bare command (grep/test/build) — verification needs no semantic judgment.
Localized edit: no new files, no new public API/schema; expected change touches <=2 existing files (estimated from proposed_fix / spec).
Not critical: task severity in {info, warning} / priority below critical tier.
Runtime demotion (statemachine.go — informational here, enforced in Go): solo honored only on validation cycle 1 — any validation failure, stuck recovery, or retry permanently demotes to full.
lane-gate.veto: A solo verdict additionally requires goal.md `## Lane` != full (and the goal's goals.yaml entry lane != full when readable). Either surface reading `full` is a prior-G5-demotion record: the veto fires and the verdict is full regardless of G1–G4 — a demoted goal can never re-enter solo. Absent/unreadable Lane data does NOT fire the veto (it blocks recorded demotions; it is not an affirmation — the fails-closed rule below applies to criteria, not to V).
lane-gate.evaluation-sources: Consume step 3.2 → severity / proposed_fix / the derived validate commands. Plan step 3d → G1 by branch condition; G2 from goal.md `## Validation Rules` + `## Investigation Config` (every investigator must be command-type/pure-command — semantic types like code-review miss G2); G3 from module scope in the research context; G4 from the goal's goals.yaml entry priority < 10 (read-only; atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe), falling back to goal.md `## Lane` == solo (consume already passed G4); neither readable → G4 fails closed → full. Veto V → goal.md `## Lane` + the goal's goals.yaml entry lane (read-only; atomic-rename writes make unlocked reads safe). Missing data → criterion fails closed. The staleness gate (consume substep 3.2) runs BEFORE G1–G4 — a stale task is failed and dropped before any lane evaluation.
lane-gate.note: Creation-seam enforcement is enum-only plus two named machine cross-checks in goal-create (internal/mcp tools_taskvisor.go GoalCreate): REJECT lane=solo with an empty validate list (G2 proxy) and WARN-LOG a lane=solo scope spanning >1 top-level directory (G3 proxy). Everything else in G1–G4 remains operator-evaluated convention at the two entry points.

investigate.xml step 2f's inline fast-path is lane-INDEPENDENT by design (B9b): ANY all-pure-command RERUN set inlines in-window, full lane included (goal-061 post-mortem, internal/taskvisor/inline.go). inv_spawned=0 on a full-lane goal whose rerun investigators are all pure-command is EXPECTED behavior, not a lane leak.

llm.mandate: NEVER take work without claiming first — task-claim precedes goal-create, no exceptions
NEVER write GOALS_FILE — goals are created ONLY via the goal-create MCP tool; the only file this command writes is MAPPING_FILE
NEVER mark a task resolved unless its mapped goal Status is "done" in GOALS_FILE — resolution is earned by the validated goal, not assumed
NEVER retry-loop a failed status update — 403/404/422 means another actor advanced the task; drop the mapping with a warning and move on
Consume is opt-in and capped at 3 per invocation — never drain the queue

step.title: Parse arguments and preflight
step.action: Tokenize $ARGUMENTS; extract MODE, N, FILTERS per the glossary. Unknown tokens → print usage and STOP.
Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and resolve API enablement (flat `apiEnabled` wins over nested `api.enabled`).

check.action: Print: "Task API is disabled — set api.enabled: true in .tmux-cli/setting.yaml and retry."

step.title: Reconcile (always runs, before listing)
step.note: FALLBACK actor: the taskvisor daemon now PUSH-resolves a mapped backend task at the moment its goal reaches a durable terminal state (goal→done ⇒ resolved, goal→failed / blocked_by=convergence-circuit-breaker ⇒ failed) and atomically drops the entry from MAPPING_FILE. Reconcile here is the FALLBACK that settles everything the push did not — offline/API-down runs, daemon crashes, and cascade-failed downstream goals — so it must keep reading the same `{task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at}` ledger shape the daemon writes. Both actors are idempotent: a push that already resolved a task simply leaves no mapping for reconcile to see.
step.action: Read MAPPING_FILE. Absent or empty → skip to step 2.
For each mapping entry, find its goal_id in GOALS_FILE and decide:

branch.action: Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=resolved, resolution={goal_id, finished_at from the goal, summary: "goal validated and completed"}.
On success: remove the mapping entry; record "task {task_id} → resolved (goal {goal_id} done)".

branch.condition: goal Status == failed, OR Status == blocked with blocked_by == convergence-circuit-breaker
branch.action: Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=failed, resolution={goal_id, reason: the goal's failed_by/blocked_by value}.
On success: remove the mapping entry; record "task {task_id} → failed (goal {goal_id} {reason})".
Keep the mapping; record as in-flight for the step-2 table.
Remove the mapping and print: "WARNING: goal {goal_id} no longer exists — task {task_id} left in its current backend state; handle manually via task-update-status."

check.action: Another actor advanced the task. Remove the mapping, print a one-line warning with the error, and continue with the next entry — never retry.

step.action: Rewrite MAPPING_FILE with the surviving entries (write the full file once, after the loop).
Print a reconcile summary line: "Reconciled: {r} resolved, {f} failed, {k} in-flight, {d} dropped." (omit when all four are 0).

step.title: List unresolved tasks
step.action: The backend filters by a single status per call. Compose "unresolved" from three task-list MCP calls: status=new, status=claimed, status=in_progress — each with FILTERS applied and limit=50.
Merge the pages, de-duplicate by id, and sort: severity (critical > warning > info), then created_at ascending (oldest first).
Use description_preview only — never call task-get for rows the user did not ask about.
Print totals per status; when any call reports total > limit, print: "more available — narrow with category=/severity= filters".

check.condition: all three calls return zero tasks
check.action: Print: "No unresolved tasks." Then STOP (consume mode has nothing to claim either).

step.title: Consume — claim and convert to goals (MODE == consume only)
step.action: Repeat up to N times (stop early when nothing claimable):

substep.title: Claim BEFORE any work

check.action: Print: "Nothing claimable (filters: {FILTERS})." and exit the consume loop.

substep.action: Use the returned task object verbatim — id, title, description, proposed_fix, expected_green_state, severity.

substep.title: Convert to a goal via goal-create

params.param: expected_green_state split into individually checkable items, plus: "Backend task {id} is satisfied: {expected_green_state first line}".

Concrete bare commands (no shell wrappers, no && chains), derived in priority order: (1) backticked/recognizable commands inside expected_green_state, (2) commands inside proposed_fix, (3) a minimal deterministic check the green state implies (a test run or a grep against the named artifact). MUST be non-empty — if no command is derivable, the task is not consumable: print "task {id} has no checkable green state — refine it on the backend first", call task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "unconsumable: no derivable validate command"}, drop it, and continue the loop.

Full task description + "## Proposed fix
" + proposed_fix + "
## Origin
Backend task {id}; resolution is marked automatically by /tmux:task-list reconcile once this goal completes."
"solo" iff the solo-lane gate passes (gate action below); on any G-miss OMIT this param entirely — absent lane = full, never pass lane: "full".

staleness-gate.title: STALENESS GATE — verify the claimed task still describes the current tree (runs AFTER the validate derivation above, BEFORE the lane gate)
staleness-gate.check: Run each derived validate command against the current tree. Exit 0 = green, non-zero = red. ALL green → the task's green state already holds: call task-update-status id status=failed resolution={reason: "unconsumable: stale — green state already holds at baseline"}, create NO goal, drop the task, continue the loop. Baseline-red is NOT sufficient proof of consumability — red can mean "fix not applied" OR "green state obsolete" (check 3 disambiguates).

check.title: Verify Evidence quotes against current files

staleness-gate.check: Pick 1–2 verbatim quotes from the task's cited Evidence (quoted text, never line numbers — line citations rot fastest). Each must still appear in the cited file. Missing quote → re-read the cited region and judge: defect GONE → fail as stale (check-1 reason path); defect MOVED → update the goal context param with current line numbers and proceed. A task with no quotable Evidence skips this check — checks 1 and 3 still gate.

goal-create requires at least one baseline-red discriminator whose red-ness is explained by the MISSING FIX, not by a contradicting design change — a red that contradicts the live design means the task's green state is obsolete and now wrong: fail as stale (check-1 reason path).
staleness-gate.note: If a stale goal slipped through anyway, the sanctioned undo is `tmux-cli taskvisor goal delete <id>` (single-goal removal; refuses a running goal). goal-prune is nuclear — it wipes ALL taskvisor goal state and is never for single-goal removal.

substep.action: Apply the solo-lane gate (the <lane-gate name="solo-lane"> block at the top of this file) — evaluated AFTER the validate-command derivation and the STALENESS GATE above (G2 judges the derived commands) and BEFORE calling goal-create: G1 from the single claimed task, G2 from the derived validate commands, G3 from proposed_fix, G4 from severity. Log one per-criterion verdict line, e.g. `lane gate: G1 ✓ G2 ✓ G3 ✗ G4 ✓ → full`. ALL of G1–G4 hold → pass `lane: "solo"` to goal-create; any miss → omit the lane param.

check.action: Print the error. The task stays claimed on the backend (there is no unclaim) — print: "task {id} remains claimed; re-run /tmux:task-list consume after fixing, or advance it manually with task-update-status." Continue the loop.

substep.title: Record mapping + mark in_progress
substep.action: Append {task_id, goal_id, title, claimed_at: now UTC} to MAPPING_FILE (create the file with a `mappings:` root on first use).
Call task-update-status with id=task_id, status=in_progress — work is queued, the claim is acted on.

step.title: Auto-start taskvisor (MODE == consume, at least one goal created)

check.condition: MODE != consume OR no goal was created this run

check.action: Print: "taskvisor is already active — {goal ids} will be scheduled on the next tick." Skip to substep 4.4.

substep.title: Ensure the daemon process is running

check.condition: restart errors (no session / no taskvisor window)
check.action: Print: "Cannot auto-start: {error}. Start a session with tmux-cli start, then run: tmux-cli taskvisor restart — the queued goals will execute on activation."
Skip to substep 4.4 (degraded — goals stay queued).

substep.title: Activate and confirm
substep.action: Call the taskvisor-start MCP tool (validates pending goals exist and writes the .tmux-cli/taskvisor-start marker; the idle daemon consumes it on its next poll).

check.action: Print the last ~10 lines of .tmux-cli/logs/taskvisor.log and: "Daemon did not activate within 30s — inspect the taskvisor window; goals remain queued."

substep.title: Reconcile reminder
substep.action: Print: "Run /tmux:task-list again after the run to reconcile results back to the backend."

execution-rules.rule: Order is fixed: reconcile → list → consume → auto-start; reconcile always runs so the rendered list is truthful
Claim before work; in_progress after goal creation; resolved/failed only from the mapped goal's terminal status
Only file written: MAPPING_FILE (full-file rewrite after reconcile, append on consume)
GOALS_FILE is read-only; goals are created exclusively through goal-create
Status-update failures (403/404/422) drop the mapping with a warning — never retried, never fatal to the run
Consume cap: 3 per invocation regardless of N
Consume always ends with the daemon executing (or a named, actionable reason why not) — queued-but-idle is a failure of this spec
The solo-lane gate is applied exactly ONCE per claimed task — here at step 3.2, upstream; executing surfaces (supervisor/investigate) only READ the lane, never re-evaluate or self-declare it
Mis-created goal undo: tmux-cli taskvisor goal delete <id> — the sanctioned single-goal removal; goal-prune is nuclear (wipes ALL goal state) and never for single-goal removal
